Source_Species.reason: RAR1 knockdown was performed in soybean plants.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods identify soybean as Glycine max (L.) Merr.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Soybean is explicitly given as Glycine max.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: RAR1 silencing was tested in L76-1988 (Rj2 rfg1) and Rpg1-b genotype plants; Methods state L76-1988 also has the Rpg1-b genotype, and Harosoy is listed as Rpg1-b.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: RAR1 itself was not shown to control glycerol-3-phosphate levels directly; G3P accumulation was measured for incompatible interactions and G3Pdh knockdown, not RAR1 knockdown.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Because linkage of RAR1 to G3P as its terminal metabolite was not directly shown in Results.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: RAR1 is expanded as required for Mla12-mediated resistance, but CHORD domains are not described in this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RAR1 knockdown permitted nodulation by incompatible rhizobia and abolished incompatible rhizobia-induced foliar resistance (Figs. 1, 2d).

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: G3P is described as mediating systemic immune signaling, but this field ties that function specifically through RAR1, which was not directly established.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: G3P accumulation was measured in leaves after incompatible rhizobia inoculation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RAR1 was tested by VIGS knockdown, nodulation phenotyping, and foliar pathogen resistance assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: RAR1 was functionally required in Rj2/Rfg1-mediated responses, but direct physical interaction with Rj2 or Rfg1 was not shown; NDR1 was tested separately.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an interpretive application statement not explicitly discussed in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: RAR1-related phenotypes were assessed under incompatible U122/U257 inoculation and Psg avrB treatment.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: RAR1 knockdown allowed undesirable nodulation by incompatible strains and compromised systemic disease resistance in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: RAR1 was directly manipulated, but 'necessary for metabolite-linked phenotype' overstates explicit linkage to G3P because RAR1-dependent metabolite data were not shown.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Points 1 and 2 are supported, but point 3 claiming RAR1 acts upstream of the G3P-associated output is not directly demonstrated.

corrections.reason: RAR1 itself was not shown to control glycerol-3-phosphate levels directly; G3P accumulation was measured for incompatible interactions and G3Pdh knockdown, not RAR1 knockdown.
Because linkage of RAR1 to G3P as its terminal metabolite was not directly shown in Results.

Source_Species.reason: NDR1 knockdown was studied in soybean.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Soybean is identified as Glycine max.

Applied_Species.reason: All NDR1 experiments were in soybean.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: NDR1 was not directly shown to regulate or produce glycerol-3-phosphate.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: This depends on unsupported specific linkage of NDR1 to G3P.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: NDR1 is explicitly named non-disease resistance 1, a disease resistance signaling component.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: NDR1 knockdown allowed active nodules by incompatible strains and abolished associated systemic foliar resistance (Figs. 1, 2d).

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Systemic immune signaling by G3P is described generally, but not directly assigned through NDR1 experimentally.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper shows leaf accumulation of G3P during incompatible rhizobia interaction.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: NDR1 was assessed by VIGS, nodulation phenotyping, and pathogen resistance assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional relationship with Rj2/Rfg1 is shown, but direct interactions are not demonstrated.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Application value is not explicitly discussed in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: NDR1-related assays involved incompatible rhizobial inoculation and Psg avrB treatment.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of NDR1 reduced disease resistance and altered rhizobial selectivity in the reported experiments.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly notes NDR1 requirement is notable because TIR-NB-LRR proteins typically recruit EDS1 rather than NDR1.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Statements 1 and 2 are supported, but linking NDR1 specifically to downstream G3P-associated signaling is inferential rather than directly shown.

corrections.reason: NDR1 was not directly shown to regulate or produce glycerol-3-phosphate.
This depends on unsupported specific linkage of NDR1 to G3P.

Gene_Name.reason: The paper studies a putative G3P dehydrogenase and later refers to GmG3Pdh knockdown.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Glyma02g38310 is explicitly identified as the putative G3P dehydrogenase and listed in Sequence accessions.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were performed in soybean.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Main experiments used L76-1988 (Rj2 rfg1), and grafting used Rj2 backgrounds.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GmG3Pdh is the studied glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase required for G3P accumulation.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper directly frames GmG3Pdh in G3P biosynthesis and SAR-associated signaling.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The gene is described as a putative/functional G3P dehydrogenase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is provided in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Functional G3Pdh is supported, but 'NAD-dependent' domain/family wording is not explicitly stated.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The substrate dihydroxyacetone phosphate is not stated in the paper.

Primary_Product.reason: The enzyme is identified as G3P dehydrogenase and linked to G3P production; G3P levels are measured as its product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is standard biochemistry for G3Pdh, but the reaction description is not explicitly given in the paper text.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper treats GmG3Pdh as directly required for biosynthesis of the target metabolite G3P.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Silencing GmG3Pdh abolishes G3P accumulation, supporting direct connection to G3P formation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: G3P was measured in leaves, petiole exudates, and root exudates.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Glyma02g38310 transcripts were expressed in foliar and root tissues and induced in leaves by U122.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same soybean system shows expression in leaves/roots and induction in leaves after incompatible inoculation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Silencing GmG3Pdh abolished leaf G3P accumulation, allowed nodulation by incompatible U122, and blocked rhizobia- and pathogen-induced systemic resistance.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper shows essential function in this context, but does not establish it as rate-limiting.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used: VIGS, metabolite assays, yeast complementation, grafting, exogenous G3P, 14C-G3P transport, nodulation, pathogen assays.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data accession GSE139303 is provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Experiments involved USDA122, USDA257, and Psg avrB as stated.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application inference, not explicitly discussed.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows G3P signaling can restrict nodulation by incompatible rhizobia, implying specificity tradeoffs; however broader tradeoffs remain interpretive.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Grafting showed shoot G3Pdh was essential; transport assays showed shoot-to-root movement during incompatible interaction and derivatives/glycerol in roots.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points are directly supported by Results.

Source_Species.reason: Rj2 is a soybean gene/trait studied in soybean genotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Incompatible Rj2 interaction is associated with G3P accumulation, but Rj2 is not a metabolic gene producing G3P.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Depends on the above indirect linkage.

Regulator_Type.reason: Rj2 is stated to encode a protein homologous to TIR-NB-LRR type resistance proteins.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TIR-NB-LRR is explicitly stated for Rj2/Rfg1.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper supports Rj2 control of incompatibility and systemic signaling associated with G3P, but not a direct control of G3P biosynthesis mechanism.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The paper presents Rj2-mediated incompatibility as genetically regulated immune recognition-like signaling.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Downstream G3P accumulation is associated, but specific regulatory targets are not directly identified.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Rj2-dependent promotion of GmG3Pdh induction is plausible but not directly tested by comparing Rj2 vs rj2 transcript induction.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Rj2 decisively determines incompatibility with U122, but 'target product' G3P wording is indirect.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Recognition of NopP by Rj2 is discussed from prior work, not tested here.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Grafting shows root genotype determines incompatibility and foliar resistance induction.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: G3P accumulation during incompatible interaction was measured in leaves and exudates.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Rj2 genotype excludes U122 and induces foliar pathogen resistance associated with incompatible interaction.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: RAR1/NDR1/HSP90/SGT1 were functionally tested, but no direct complex with Rj2 was shown.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Natural genotype comparison, VIGS of signaling components, grafting, nodulation assays, pathogen resistance assays, and metabolite measurements were used in Rj2-related analyses.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: RAR1 and NDR1 were required functionally, but direct protein interactions with Rj2 were not demonstrated.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq accession GSE139303 is provided and transcriptome analyses included Rj2 plants.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Rj2-mediated responses were tested after root inoculation with incompatible U122.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This breeding interpretation is not explicitly discussed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper discusses/exhibits exclusion of certain rhizobial strains by Rj2, implying compatibility tradeoffs.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Grafting experiments directly show root recognition but requirement for shoot G3P biosynthesis and return transport for exclusion.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported by genotype comparison, metabolite data, grafting, and knockdown analyses.

Source_Species.reason: Rfg1 is a soybean gene/trait studied in soybean genotypes.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper lists L82-257 as rj2 Rfg1 in Methods, not L82-2024.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Rfg1-mediated incompatibility is associated with systemic resistance, but G3P was not directly measured specifically in the Rfg1/U257 context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Depends on the indirect linkage above.

Regulator_Type.reason: Rj2 and Rfg1 encoded proteins are stated to show homology to TIR-NB-LRR resistance proteins.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TIR-NB-LRR is explicitly mentioned for Rj2/Rfg1.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Systemic signaling is shown, but G3P-associated control by Rfg1 specifically was not dissected mechanistically.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Rfg1 is presented as mediating genetically regulated incompatibility through immune recognition-like processes.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Specific downstream targets were not identified for Rfg1.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Rfg1 determines incompatibility to U257/USDA205, but direct influence on G3P as a target product is not shown.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Incompatible rhizobial strain recognition is central to Rfg1 function in the paper/background.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Root-genotype dependence was directly graft-tested for Rj2, not Rfg1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf G3P accumulation was shown for U122 in Rj2 plants, not specifically for Rfg1/U257.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: rj2 Rfg1 plants exclude U257 and show foliar resistance when preinoculated with incompatible U257 (Fig. 2b).

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: RAR1/NDR1 dependence was tested in compatibility backgrounds involving Rj2 and Rfg1, but no direct Rfg1 complex was shown.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Genotype comparison, VIGS of RAR1/NDR1, nodulation assays, and pathogen resistance assays were used.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct physical interactions with RAR1 or NDR1 were shown.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Rfg1-related responses were tested with incompatible U257 inoculation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application not explicitly discussed.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Rfg1 blocks nodulation by certain strains, implying inoculant compatibility tradeoffs.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper focuses mechanistically more on Rj2/G3P, so Rfg1 is less dissected but treated as a parallel incompatibility regulator.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Points 1 and 2 are supported, but point 3 about shared ETI-like signaling components for the Rfg1 response is suggestive rather than directly demonstrated specifically for U257 in the same detail.

corrections.reason: The paper lists L82-257 as rj2 Rfg1 in Methods, not L82-2024.
